mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, it signals labor market deterioration and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable consumer prices indicates robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 percent year-over-year, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when monetary policy is tightening.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 150 thousand confirm sustained economic momentum and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 150 thousand in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in over 55 percent of months where PAYEMS exceeds the 150 thousand threshold, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: High federal funds rates combined with slowing real GDP growth indicate a restrictive monetary policy environment that suppresses corporate profitability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive returns in 60 percent of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when GDPC1 growth remains above 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal increased construction activity and consumer confidence in long-term economic stability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns, or the signal becomes negative when mortgage rates exceed 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid price increases alongside stagnant industrial production indicate supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL accelerates but INDPRO does not decline, or the signal shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample testing.
